title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in New Hampshire
description: This race is for New Hampshire’s Class II U.S. Senate seat, which is open because incumbent Democrat Jeanne Shaheen announced she will not seek reelection in 2026. New Hampshire has been closely contested in recent federal elections, so the seat is expected to be highly competitive and important to control of the Senate. The main matchup is between Democrat Chris Pappas, a sitting U.S. representative, and Republican John E. Sununu, a former U.S. senator returning to the ballot after many years away from elected office. Sources: https://www.shaheen.senate.gov/news/press/after-30-years-making-a-difference-jeanne-shaheen-announces-she-will-not-seek-reelection-in-2026, https://scholars.unh.edu/survey_center_polls/922/, https://newhampshirebulletin.com/2025/10/23/nearly-two-decades-later-john-e-sununu-wants-new-hampshire-to-send-him-back-to-the-u-s-senate/

polling.pollster: University of New Hampshire Survey Center

candidates.name: Chris Pappas
candidates.summary: Chris Pappas is a Democratic U.S. representative from New Hampshire’s 1st Congressional District and the party’s leading candidate for the state’s open Senate seat in 2026. His campaign emphasizes his Manchester roots, small-business background, and focus on affordability, health care, and protecting New Hampshire’s interests in Washington.

summary_sources.title: Chris Pappas for Senate | Official Campaign Website

career_history.organization: U.S. House of Representatives, New Hampshire's 1st Congressional District
career_history.description: Represents eastern New Hampshire in Congress.

career_history.title: Executive Councilor
career_history.description: Served on the five-member state executive council.

career_history.title: Business owner
career_history.description: Worked in and helped run the family restaurant business in Manchester.

voting_record.bill_description: Eliminated certain Social Security benefit reductions for public-sector retirees.

Healthcare.stance: Pappas says he wants to lower health care costs, protect and expand ACA premium tax credits, oppose Medicaid cuts, cap insulin costs, allow Medicare to negotiate drug prices, and expand telehealth.

sources.title: Chris Pappas for Senate’s Tax Cuts and Lower Costs Blueprint Spotlights Efforts to Make Health Care Affordable
Chris Pappas for Senate | Official Campaign Website

Economy.stance: Pappas says he is focused on lowering costs for working families and small businesses, including food, energy, and drug costs. His campaign also says he supports cracking down on corporate and AI-driven price gouging and protecting nutrition assistance.

sources.title: Meet Chris | Chris Pappas for Senate
ICYMI: Manchester Ink Link — Pappas Talks About Food Prices at Downtown Discussion
Pappas launches campaign for U.S. Senate | New Hampshire Public Radio

Climate/Energy.stance: Pappas says he is committed to combating climate change, promoting clean energy, and protecting natural resources. He supports maintaining clean energy incentives and tax credits and opposes rolling back environmental protections.

sources.title: Energy & Environment | Congressman Chris Pappas - House.gov

Reproductive Rights.stance: Pappas’s campaign describes him as a supporter of abortion access and reproductive freedom. It says he has backed Planned Parenthood funding and related legislation to protect abortion and birth control access.

sources.title: ROUNDUP: Reproductive Rights Leaders Rally Behind Chris Pappas on Anniversary of Roe v. Wade
Planned Parenthood Action Fund Announces Endorsement of Reproductive Rights Champion Congressman Chris Pappas for Senate in 2026

Immigration.stance: Pappas has called for congressional oversight of ICE and for preserving local control over immigration-related decisions. He has also said some ICE funding should be redirected to local law enforcement.

sources.title: ICYMI: Congressman Chris Pappas Joins Raging Moderates Podcast to Discuss Key Issues and Senate Campaign
Pappas Calls on Congressional Leaders to Protect Local Control for Communities like Merrimack in DHS Funding Negotiations
Chris Pappas targets ICE funds, calls for refocus | CloseUp - WMUR

Guns & Safety.stance: Pappas supports universal background checks, closing loopholes such as the Charleston loophole and bump-stock loophole, and safe-storage measures like Ethan’s Law.

sources.title: Pappas Backs Three Gun Violence Prevention Bills
Chris Pappas | GIFFORDS
Christopher Pappas - profile history | Citizens Count

Foreign Policy.stance: Pappas says U.S. foreign policy should be values-driven, emphasizing alliances, democracy, and human rights. He has also criticized open-ended military commitments and said the U.S. should use power carefully.

sources.title: ROUNDUP: Chris Pappas for Senate Continues to Build Broad Coalition of Key Endorsements
ICYMI: Congressman Chris Pappas Joins Raging Moderates Podcast to Discuss Key Issues and Senate Campaign

Social Justice.stance: Pappas has backed police accountability reforms, including the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act, and supports LGBTQ+ equality. He has also opposed legislation he described as harmful and discriminatory toward transgender students.

sources.title: Congressman Pappas Statement on Passage of the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act
Pappas Statement on Executive Orders Advancing LGBTQ+ Equality
LGBTQ and Equality | Congressman Chris Pappas

Education.stance: Pappas says he is a product of public schools and has emphasized investment in people and families. His campaign materials reviewed here do not lay out a detailed K-12 or higher-education agenda.

sources.title: Meet Chris | Chris Pappas for Senate
ICYMI: Manchester Ink Link — Pappas Talks About Food Prices at Downtown Discussion

Tech & AI.stance: Pappas has engaged on AI-related policy through Congress, including legislation addressing deepfakes and other digital harms. His emphasis appears to be on regulating harmful uses of AI and protecting consumers.

sources.title: All bills - live.house.gov, U.S. House of Representatives
[PDF] Committee on Energy and Commerce Hearing entitled “TikTok”
Buchanan Introduces Two Bills to Protect Our Nation's Children from Modern Threats

Election Reform.stance: Pappas’s public campaign materials reviewed here do not lay out a specific election-reform agenda. Available sources emphasize broader governance and constituent-service priorities rather than a detailed election-reform proposal.

sources.title: Chris Pappas for Senate | Official Campaign Website
Issues | Congressman Chris Pappas

Local Issues.stance: Pappas presents himself as focused on New Hampshire-specific concerns, especially infrastructure, affordability, health care, and the opioid crisis. His House issues page says he is fighting for federal investment in New Hampshire’s roads, bridges, and waterways and highlights Medicaid expansion and local public-health needs.

sources.title: Issues | Congressman Chris Pappas
ROUNDUP: Pete Buttigieg Campaigns with Chris Pappas Highlights Stakes of Must-Win 2026 NH Senate Race

candidates.name: John E. Sununu
candidates.summary: John E. Sununu is a Republican former U.S. senator and former U.S. representative from New Hampshire who is seeking a return to the Senate in 2026. His campaign presents him as an experienced, fiscally conservative candidate with prior statewide and federal service.

summary_sources.title: SUNUNU, John E. | US House of Representatives

Healthcare.stance: Sununu’s 2026 campaign website says he will focus on affordability and says he has defended the rights and privacy of ordinary Americans, but it does not lay out a detailed health care platform in the sources reviewed.

sources.title: John Sununu for U.S. Senate

Economy.stance: Sununu says his campaign will be focused on the economy, jobs, and affordability. He says he wants to lower inflation and the cost of housing, health care, and energy, and frames his approach as fiscally conservative and opposed to tax increases.

sources.title: About | John Sununu for U.S. Senate
John E. Sununu Announces Run for U.S. Senate: Bringing the New Hampshire Way Back to Washington

Climate/Energy.stance: Sununu’s campaign materials reviewed here do not provide a detailed climate or energy platform. The available sources emphasize affordability, low taxes, and working with the Trump administration rather than specific climate policy proposals.

sources.title: John Sununu launches 2026 GOP Senate campaign in New Hampshire race | Fox News
Republican John Sununu running for US Senate in New Hampshire

Reproductive Rights.stance: Sununu’s current campaign materials reviewed do not present a detailed reproductive-rights platform. His past congressional record and contemporary reporting indicate opposition to abortion-rights expansion and abortion-related funding measures.

sources.title: John Sununu for U.S. Senate
ROUNDUP: Reproductive Rights Leaders Rally Behind Chris Pappas on Anniversary of Roe v. Wade
Republican John Sununu running for US Senate in New Hampshire

Immigration.stance: Sununu’s current campaign materials reviewed here do not lay out a detailed immigration platform. The launch coverage focused on taxes, affordability, and working with Trump rather than immigration.

sources.title: John Sununu launches 2026 GOP Senate campaign in New Hampshire race | Fox News

Guns & Safety.stance: Sununu’s public record shows he vetoed three gun bills in 2019, including universal background checks, a waiting period, and a school-property firearms restriction, saying they would not prevent harm and that New Hampshire’s laws fit its culture of responsible gun ownership and individual freedom.

sources.title: Sununu Vetoes Gun Bills, Says They Wouldn't Stop 'Evil Individuals From Doing Harm'
About | John Sununu for U.S. Senate

Foreign Policy.stance: Sununu says he wants to work with President Trump and the administration to get things done, but the available sources do not provide a detailed foreign-policy agenda. His launch message emphasized pragmatism and helping New Hampshire rather than specific positions on global issues.

sources.title: John Sununu launches 2026 GOP Senate campaign in New Hampshire race | Fox News

Social Justice.stance: Sununu’s campaign biography emphasizes bipartisan mental health reforms, protections for Americans with disabilities, and defending privacy and rights against government overreach, but it does not lay out a detailed social-justice agenda.

sources.title: About | John Sununu for U.S. Senate
Sununu Vetoes Gun Bills, Says They Wouldn't Stop 'Evil Individuals From Doing Harm'

Education.stance: Sununu’s campaign bio says he has served on charitable, educational, and nonprofit boards, but it does not present a detailed education policy agenda. The clearest education-related detail in the reviewed material is his family background, including a mother who served as chair of the Salem School Board.

sources.title: About | John Sununu for U.S. Senate

Tech & AI.stance: Sununu says he built a career in high tech, engineering, consulting, and manufacturing, and his campaign frames that background as part of the experience he would bring to Washington. The sources reviewed do not show a detailed AI policy agenda.

sources.title: About | John Sununu for U.S. Senate
John Sununu launches 2026 GOP Senate campaign in New Hampshire race | Fox News

Election Reform.stance: Sununu’s current campaign materials reviewed here do not lay out a detailed election-reform platform. The available sources emphasize a broad message of lowering the temperature and restoring functional government rather than a specific election-reform proposal.

sources.title: John Sununu for U.S. Senate
Nearly two decades later, John E. Sununu wants New Hampshire to send him back to the U.S. Senate

Local Issues.stance: Sununu’s campaign frames him as a New Hampshire-first candidate who will bring a practical, results-oriented approach to Washington. The campaign site says he will put New Hampshire first and focus on affordability and functional government.

sources.title: John Sununu for U.S. Senate
Nearly two decades later, John E. Sununu wants New Hampshire to send him back to the U.S. Senate

flags.concern: John E. Sununu served as Governor of New Hampshire (2009–2017), a significant statewide executive position that is completely absent from his career history. This is a material omission given his campaign narrative about 'returning' to public service.
flags.suggestion: Add a governor entry covering 2009–2017 with description of executive duties and key initiatives.

flags.concern: Sununu's issue positions are dramatically under-detailed compared to Pappas. Of 12 issue areas, Sununu has 9 marked 'low' or 'medium' confidence with single-source or campaign-only sourcing, vs. Pappas's consistent 'high' confidence with 2–3 sources per issue. This creates a substantial fairness problem and suggests incomplete research.
flags.suggestion: Substantially expand sourcing for Sununu across Healthcare, Climate/Energy, Reproductive Rights, Immigration, Foreign Policy, Social Justice, Education, and Tech & AI. Consult news archives, legislative records, and campaign materials to establish comparable depth.

flags.concern: Marked 'low' confidence with only campaign website as source. No detail on ACA, drug pricing, Medicaid, or specific health-care positions.
flags.suggestion: Search for Sununu interviews, news coverage, and past congressional votes on health legislation to document his actual platform.

flags.concern: Marked 'low' confidence. Sources reference Trump administration alignment and affordability but provide no actual climate or energy policy position. This is a major omission for a 2026 race.
flags.suggestion: Identify Sununu's statements on renewable energy, fossil fuels, environmental regulation, and climate action. Review any past House/Senate votes on energy bills.

flags.concern: Marked 'medium' confidence. The stance relies partly on inference from past voting record (S.3 Partial-Birth Abortion Ban, 2003) and opposition-campaign language rather than current Sununu statements. His 2026 campaign position is unclear.
flags.suggestion: Seek explicit 2026 campaign statements or recent interviews clarifying his position on abortion access, Roe reversal, and reproductive policy.

flags.concern: Marked 'low' confidence with only one news source (launch coverage). No detail on specific immigration positions despite it being a major 2026 campaign issue.
flags.suggestion: Research Sununu's past House votes on immigration, his recent statements on border, asylum, H-1B, and other topics, and his campaign messaging on immigration.

flags.concern: Marked 'low' confidence. Stance is vague ('work with Trump') with minimal substantive content. No position on Ukraine, Middle East, NATO, or other major foreign-policy questions.
flags.suggestion: Document Sununu's past Senate votes on foreign aid, military intervention, and trade. Seek recent interviews or campaign statements on specific foreign-policy priorities.

flags.concern: Marked 'low' confidence. Stance mentions mental health and disability rights but provides no detail on police reform, racial justice, LGBTQ+ rights, or voting rights—key social-justice topics.
flags.suggestion: Research Sununu's record on criminal justice, civil rights, and LGBTQ+ issues. Include any relevant vetoes or legislative positions.

flags.concern: Marked 'medium' confidence with minimal sourcing. States family connection to education but no policy detail on K–12 funding, higher education, student debt, or education priorities.
flags.suggestion: Add Sununu's statements on education policy, any House/Senate votes on education bills, and campaign priorities for schools and colleges.

flags.concern: Marked 'medium' confidence. Stance relies on Sununu's personal background in engineering/tech rather than documented policy positions on AI regulation, data privacy, or tech policy.
flags.suggestion: Find specific statements or positions on AI policy, data privacy, tech regulation, or infrastructure tech initiatives from his campaign or recent interviews.

flags.concern: Top donors appear to be historical (career-total) from opensecrets.org rather than 2026 campaign-cycle donors. For a candidate just launching in 2025–2026, these figures may not reflect current fundraising patterns.
flags.suggestion: Clarify the time period (career vs. 2026 cycle) in the source or update to reflect the most recent FEC filings for the 2026 election cycle.

flags.concern: Same issue: top donors appear to be career-total figures. Clarify cycle or update to 2026 if available.
flags.suggestion: Specify whether these are career totals or 2026-cycle figures, and update to latest FEC data if available.

flags.concern: Only one poll is provided (UNH Jan 2026), dated quite recently. For a competitive race, more polls—especially from different pollsters—would strengthen the polling section. Also, Jan 2026 poll is within 3 months of the profile access date, limiting temporal breadth.
flags.suggestion: If available, add additional polls from other organizations (WMUR, Emerson, etc.) to provide a fuller picture of the race trajectory.

flags.concern: Pappas image is from 116th Congress (2019–2021). Sununu image is undated. For a 2026 race, more recent photos would be preferable.
flags.suggestion: Update to 117th or 118th Congress images if available, or verify that images are appropriately recent and representative.

flags.concern: Only three historical votes (2003–2006) are included, all from Sununu's Senate tenure more than 15 years ago. No recent House record is documented (he served 1997–2003).
flags.suggestion: If seeking to document his legislative record, consider including a mix of representative votes across his House and Senate service, or note in the profile that he has been out of elected office since 2009.

flags.concern: Title uses 'U.S. Senate election' but profile appears to reflect 2026 cycle. Minor: confirm the standard title format aligns with other race profiles.
flags.suggestion: No change needed if this is the standard format, but verify consistency.

flags.concern: Description is accurate and well-sourced. However, it could note that the race is also important for broader Senate control given New Hampshire's swing-state status. Minor enhancement only.
flags.suggestion: Optional: add language about Senate control implications.

reviews.summary: This is a well-structured and generally well-sourced profile with strong coverage of Chris Pappas's detailed policy positions across 11 issue areas. However, there are significant asymmetries in the depth and confidence levels for John Sununu's positions—most are marked 'low' or 'medium' confidence with minimal sourcing—that undermine fairness and completeness. Additionally, there is a critical factual error regarding Sununu's gubernatorial role, and several source issues require attention before publication.

flags.concern: The career history mixes the record of former Senator John E. Sununu with the record of his brother, former Governor Chris Sununu.
flags.suggestion: Verify that the candidate is indeed John E. Sununu (the former Senator) and ensure that only his actions—not those of the former Governor—are listed.

flags.concern: The stance incorrectly attributes vetoes of gun bills in 2019 to John E. Sununu. John E. Sununu left the Senate in 2009; the 2019 vetoes were issued by then-Governor Chris Sununu.
flags.suggestion: Remove the reference to the 2019 vetoes as they are not actions taken by the candidate described.

flags.concern: Potential inaccuracy; the biographical details listed for John E. Sununu (the Senator) and the confusion with his brother suggest a need for a re-verification of the academic history provided.
flags.suggestion: Cross-reference against the official Senate historical archives specifically for John E. Sununu.

flags.concern: Listing 'State of New Hampshire' as a top donor to a congressional campaign is likely an error in data interpretation from OpenSecrets (often miscategorizing state employee contributions or organizational labels).
flags.suggestion: Review the OpenSecrets data source to confirm if this represents individual donors from the state or if the categorization is misleading.

reviews.summary: The profile is well-structured and provides a comprehensive overview of the candidates. However, it contains significant factual inconsistencies regarding the political identity of John E. Sununu. The profile conflates the former U.S. Senator John E. Sununu with the recent former Governor of New Hampshire, Chris Sununu, leading to a confusion of their respective career histories and records (specifically regarding the 2019 vetoes).

flags.concern: The stance has low confidence and is based on a single source from the candidate's campaign website, lacking depth and corroboration from more detailed policy statements or neutral analyses.
flags.suggestion: Incorporate additional sources, such as recent interviews or policy papers from Sununu, to provide a more comprehensive view.

flags.concern: The stance is marked as low confidence and relies on news articles that do not directly address Sununu's positions, resulting in incomplete coverage of this key issue.
flags.suggestion: Add specific sources from Sununu's campaign or past records to outline his views more clearly.

flags.concern: One source is from Chris Pappas's campaign website, which introduces potential bias and indirect relevance, as it discusses opposition rather than Sununu's own positions.
flags.suggestion: Replace with primary sources directly from Sununu's materials or credible neutral outlets to ensure impartiality.

flags.concern: The stance has low confidence and is based solely on campaign launch coverage, omitting any detailed policy history or current statements, which leaves the profile incomplete.
flags.suggestion: Include more recent or specific sources, such as Sununu's voting record or public speeches, for a fuller picture.

flags.concern: The stance is low confidence and lacks specificity, relying on general news reports without tying into Sununu's historical positions or detailed campaign plans.
flags.suggestion: Enhance with sources from his Senate tenure or recent policy discussions to improve accuracy and completeness.

flags.concern: The poll date is set in 2026, which may not align with current realities, potentially indicating fictional or simulated data that could mislead users.
flags.suggestion: Verify and update with real-time or historically accurate polling data if available, or clearly note it as projected.

reviews.summary: The profile is generally accurate and unbiased, with strong sourcing for Chris Pappas's background and issues, drawing from official campaign sites, government records, and credible news outlets. However, it falls short in completeness for John E. Sununu, where several issues have low-confidence stances and rely on limited or indirect sources, potentially leaving gaps in a balanced representation.
